utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good morning

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good morning thanks doctor doctor cooper i'm i'm you know i'm a little i'm sad to be in here but you know thanks for taking me in i appreciate it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure absolutely what can i help you with today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so you know i've been dealing with my asthma and like i tried to join sports but it's really kind of it's getting hard you know and i i i just wonder if there's something that can be done because i really do like playing water polo

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but i'm having difficulty breathing sometimes i've had to like you know stop matches and sit on the side just to kind of like catch my breath and use my inhaler so i was wondering if there was something we could do about it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then like i'm kind of a little bit worried i think my mood is getting a little a little worrisome and i i wanted to explore like what my options were

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay let's talk about the asthma first so what inhaler are you using now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have an albuterol inhaler

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and when when you're having trouble it's usually just around sports that is it keeping you up at night

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i do n't really like wake up at night a lot typically like it's sports like you know if i'm doing anything like crazy aerobic or like running or anything i do notice that if any if i'm around smoke i do start coughing a little bit but most of the time it's sports

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and can you describe a little bit for me what happens

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i start to yeah no so i start to feel like there is like some phlegm building up in my in my throat and i start coughing like my chest gets tight i start wheezing and i just have to sit down or else i'm gon na get like lightheaded too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then when you use your inhaler

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does it does it alleviate the problem

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so yeah it helps with that like phlegm feeling you know but i still i still have to sit down you know and like breathe and then the thing that i hate about that inhaler is i start getting like shaky is that supposed to be happening

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes that is unfortunately normal and a side effect with the inhaler

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you use you're using two puffs of the inhaler

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for the symptoms

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then you sit down and does it does it get better within about fifteen minutes or so

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah it does but you know i had to like step out of the the pool to make that happen i'm hoping that there is something else we can do

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you ever taken any daily medications for your asthma an inhaler or singulair or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i i just use my inhaler whenever i have an attack

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so that's something we might wan na consider but how often is it happening

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: pretty much every time i do any kind of aerobic workout

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and outside of physical activity you're not having any problems

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah there's that part where like if i'm around somebody who has been smoking a lot or is currently smoking but i usually just step away i do n't even like to be around them you know that makes sense

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright well we will look at that tell me about the mood issues you are having

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah so one of the reasons i got into like trying to get into sports is like i feel like you know you you feel a lot more energized and a lot you know happier but like lately i've just been kinda stressed out you know like i have i have like sats that i need to study for i've got like all these ap classes you know there's just it i feel like there's a lot of pressure and you know like i get it but there are times where i'm just like really down and i i do n't really know what else i can do

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that makes sense any any difficulty with focusing or you're having difficulty retaining information or is it more feeling sad not having motivation

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i think it's like a lot of sadness a lot of like you know i do n't really i kinda feel like you know i do n't really like want to do anything you know my friends will go out and i'll just be like i'd rather be at home i am really tired a lot too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well let me let me go ahead and check you out

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then we can talk a little bit more

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na take a listen to your heart and lungs

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and everything sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let me take a look at your eyes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm and in your ears everything looks okay have you had any problems with allergies you have seasonal allergies or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i think so yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do see just a little bit of fluid in the ears

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm gon na look in your mouth too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and throat looks fine no tonsils

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lem me go ahead and have you lay back on the table and i'll take a listen to your stomach

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: everything sounds okay i'm gon na feel around just to make sure everything feels normal

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: everything feels fine and i'm gon na check reflexes and they're all normal

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: awesome

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's really hard to do with actual patient so in terms of the asthma i think we could try a daily medication since it looks like you might be having a little bit of allergies maybe we can try some singulair

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and start with that once you are on that daily and you can continue to use the albuterol inhaler those side effects unfortunately you're right it's it's just one of the expected side effects with an albuterol inhaler i would recommend just what you're doing just sit down for a little bit after you take it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we will get you started on the singulair probably within about a month you should see a difference so i will have you come back in about six weeks and follow up and see how you're doing with that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: in terms of the mood is this new for you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i think so like when i started this year

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and it sounds like related to school expectations and the stress with saps and all of that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay let's consider having you start seeing a therapist i think that would be a good place to start

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we will do some screening questionnaires and and then follow up in a couple weeks on that too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright sounds like a plan okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

detail_list.summary: Asthma
The patient reports difficulty breathing during sports and the need to use an inhaler.
Mood
The doctor asks what inhaler the patient is currently using.
The patient uses an albuterol inhaler.
The doctor asks if the asthma symptoms occur at night.
The patient reports symptoms during aerobic exercise and coughing when exposed to smoke.
The doctor asks the patient to describe her asthma symptoms.
The patient reports phlegm, coughing, chest tightness, wheezing, and lightheadedness.
The doctor asks if the inhaler alleviates the symptoms.
The patient reports the inhaler helps with phlegm but causes shakiness.
The doctor confirms that shakiness is a normal side effect of the albuterol inhaler.
The patient uses 2 puffs of albuterol as needed.
The doctor asks if symptoms improve within 15 minutes of using the inhaler.
The patient confirms symptoms improve after 15 minutes.
The doctor asks if the patient has ever taken daily asthma medication.
The patient reports only using the inhaler for attacks.
The doctor asks how often the asthma symptoms occur.
The patient reports symptoms occur during every aerobic workout.
The doctor asks if the patient has problems outside of physical activity.
The patient reports symptoms only when exposed to smoke.
The doctor asks the patient to describe her mood issues.
The patient reports feeling stressed due to school and feeling down.
The doctor asks if the patient has difficulty focusing or if she feels sad and unmotivated.
The patient reports sadness, lack of motivation, and fatigue.
The doctor declares an intention to examine the heart and lungs.
The doctor reports the heart and lung exam is normal.
The doctor asks if the patient has seasonal allergies.
The patient confirms having seasonal allergies.
The doctor notes mild fluid in the ears.
The doctor notes the throat is normal and tonsils are absent.
The doctor declares an intention to examine the stomach.
The doctor reports normal bowel sounds and abdominal palpation.
The doctor reports normal reflexes.
The doctor recommends starting Singulair for asthma.
The doctor advises continuing the albuterol inhaler as needed.
The patient will follow up in 6 weeks.
The doctor asks if the mood issues are new.
The patient reports the mood issues started this year.
The doctor recommends the patient see a therapist.
The patient will complete a screening questionnaire and follow up in a couple of weeks.
